parsed_candidate.joint_topic: Continual Capability Gains Compounding Into Broad AI Impact
parsed_candidate.thesis: Iterative pretraining and fine-tuning cycles that steadily lower model loss causally produce emergent capabilities whose cumulative breadth, rather than any single jump, drives economy-wide AI impact. We can test whether per-iteration capability gains predict downstream task-transfer breadth.
parsed_candidate.mechanism: Repeated optimization across successive model generations induces incremental loss reductions that activate latent capabilities, and the accumulation of these activated capabilities transforms narrow gains into broad cross-domain impact.
parsed_candidate.primary_quote: the models just kind of get better over time

iterative model improvement downstream task transfer breadth
emergent abilities loss reduction capability accumulation
compounding capability gains broad economic impact of AI models

model performance over time benchmark capability breadth

prompt:
You are merging two research "atoms" into ONE candidate paradigm-shift research niche in AI / LLMs.

ATOM A (id TALK_E1_A04, source podcast): "Basically, you know, AI is gonna be, like, really big and obviously change everything, I mean, sort of."
ATOM B (id TALK_E1_A05, source podcast): "Right? Like, the models just kind of get better over time, you know, it's wild, okay so."

Combine the mechanism in ATOM A with the mechanism/principle in ATOM B into a single, concrete, testable research direction.

Output ONLY a fenced ```json block (no prose before or after) with EXACTLY these fields:
{
  "joint_topic": "<=12 word title of the merged niche",
  "thesis": "1-2 sentence concrete research thesis",
  "mechanism": "1-2 sentences describing the causal mechanism; MUST use concrete causal verbs such as induces, produces, causes, transforms, regulates, activates, inhibits, routes",
  "operator": "a short relation label such as INDUCES or TRANSFORMS; do NOT use ANALOGY_TRANSFERS_TO_OPEN",
  "primary_quote": "a quote of at least 30 characters copied EXACTLY (verbatim, character-for-character) from ATOM A's text or ATOM B's text; do not paraphrase or invent",
  "verification_plan": [{"source": "<name>", "query": "<search query>"}]
}

Rules:
- primary_quote MUST be an exact substring of one atom's text. Copy it; do not invent.
- verification_plan MUST list at least 5 entries with 5 DISTINCT source names.
- Output nothing except the json block.
